rulings.Case Title: LEONID PINSKI VS. ALEXANDER VILDERMAN DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Req. That Court Appoint A Neutral Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, October 18, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT VILDERMAN DDS, ALEXANDER, DBA CITY DENTAL Petition To Req. That Court Appoint A Neutral Arbitrator - Defendants¿ Petition to Appoint Neutral Arbitrator is granted. Per CCP 1281.6, the parties shall meet and confer so that they may submit a list of no fewer than eight proposed arbitrators (with each side submitting at least four) no later than October 25. None of the persons listed as a proposed arbitrator may be currently or in past affiliated with ADR Services, Inc., previously been a judicial officer of the San Mateo Superior Court, or have served as an arbitrator, judge or an attorney in any case where Mr. Pinski, Ms. Pinksi, Dr. Vilderman, Ms. Duggan, Mr. Roberts or Judge Kemp have been a party or served as a judge or an attorney within the last five years. Upon receipt of the list, the court will then issue a notice by mail to counsel nominating five persons from the list and the parties will have ten days to agree on an arbitrator, whether from the nominees or not, and if they fail to do so, the court will court will appoint one of the nominees as the third arbitrator. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. GUERDAT VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 18, 2011, Line 15, DEFENDANT SALLY UNTERBERGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to all claims that are the subject of them motion except for the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary, which is sustained without leave to amend. The failure to attach exhibits requires a new complaint. Further, the first cause of action for partition still does not sufficiently plead compliance with the conditions of the contract for the property to be sold. There is no basis for the allegation that the defendant has a fiduciary relationship with the plaintiff. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
